mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that historically precedes equity drawdowns, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's hit rate falls below 45 percent over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising consumer credit and a federal funds rate above 3.5 percent indicates excessive leverage being serviced at restrictive rates, which compresses corporate margins and equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns in high-leverage/high-rate regimes do not underperform baseline by at least 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized, a simultaneous decline in the housing starts rate signals a reallocation of capital away from residential investment toward commercial activity, which historically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The conditional forward return differential between high INDPRO/low HOUST regimes and the baseline is less than 0.5 percent over a 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP growth rate consistently expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days regardless of the prevailing interest rate environment.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in quarters with GDP growth deceleration are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, real borrowing costs become sufficiently restrictive to trigger a compression in equity multiples, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread threshold fails to predict drawdowns with more than 55 percent accuracy over a 10-year walk-forward test, or forward returns in high-rate periods are not statistically different from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where payroll employment growth exceeds 200,000 per month but real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5 percent, wage-push inflation forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which historically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or the forward return differential between the specified regime and the baseline is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects resilient aggregate supply capacity that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year correlation between INDPRO momentum and SPX 20d returns falls below 0.15, or forward returns in months of declining INDPRO are not significantly negative over a 3-year rolling window.
